design_goal: Give a security admin a guided, trustworthy, and operationally complete flow to configure and launch a multi-channel red team campaign — with no compliance surprises, no ambiguous delivery outcomes, and full visibility into who was reached, on which channel, and what happened next.
user_segments: security leadership / CISO (approver, if dual-auth confirmed)
constraints: v1 channels: SMS and WhatsApp only; vishing excluded from v1 design scope
Vishing architecture (self-serve vs. managed service) unresolved — no vishing UI designed
WhatsApp Business API feasibility for custom messages unconfirmed — design channel card with both states
Stillsuit DS v2 wizard pattern — 8 steps, back navigation preserves form state
Three new DS component patterns required: Compliance Pre-flight step, per-channel coverage indicator, individual targeting people-search
Individual targeting is net-new UX not present in simulation flows
Risk pipeline isolation vs. integration unresolved — strategy assumes isolated by default
Template library model (shared vs. separate) unconfirmed
Legal review required for compliance acknowledgment copy in Step 8
selected_strategy: Option B — Dedicated Red Team section with purpose-built 8-step wizard
selected_strategy_rationale: Red team campaigns are adversarially distinct from training simulations — different compliance model, different remediation defaults, potentially different risk pipeline. A shared wizard produces ambiguity at every step (Lucy's Expert Mode anti-pattern). A dedicated section enables clean RBAC separation, purpose-built compliance gating as a first-class wizard step, and remediation suppression as a natural default. Mirrors the SMS phishing architecture decision (Option B won there for the same reasons). Extensible to Viber, Telegram, vishing by adding channel cards in Step 1 without rewiring the wizard.

alternative_strategies.name: Option A — Red Team as extension of existing campaign types
alternative_strategies.summary: Add a Red Team type selector to the existing Simulations wizard. All campaign types share one wizard and one campaign list.
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: Compliance model for red team is materially different from simulations — shared wizard creates ambiguity. Individual targeting and per-channel coverage indicator require wizard step surgery. Mixed campaign lists create reporting confusion. Lucy's implementation of this approach (smishing behind Expert Mode) is the canonical anti-pattern in competitor research.

alternative_strategies.name: Option C — Unified Campaign Launcher with channel/type selector at step 0
alternative_strategies.summary: Redesign the entire campaign creation experience to unify Email, Smishing, and Red Team under one entry point.
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: Correct long-term direction but wrong v1 scope. Red team and simulation compliance models are different enough that a unified wizard requires conditional branching at nearly every step. Requires all channel types to be production-ready simultaneously.

tradeoffs: Separate navigation adds a surface — admins cannot see red team campaigns alongside simulations in one unified list without a future cross-type campaign view (Should priority, same tradeoff accepted for SMS phishing section)
8-step wizard with conditional branching (single vs. multi-channel) is more complex than the 7-step SMS phishing wizard — each step needs both variants
Risk pipeline isolation assumption may need to be revisited if PM confirms integrated pipeline — detail view design forks on this answer
Three new DS component patterns required before Figma work can begin on Steps 2 and 4 — creates a design dependency on DS review
assumptions: Red team results are isolated from the simulation risk score pipeline (no risk delta column in detail view) — verify with PM
WhatsApp Business API supports custom message sends for red team lures — verify with Eng before designing Step 3
Remediation default should be suppressed for red team campaigns to preserve adversarial exercise integrity
Vishing is out of v1 scope — channel card shows Coming soon state
Individual targeting (named user list) is in v1 scope alongside group targeting
open_issues: Risk score pipeline: isolated or integrated with simulations? Strategy branches on this answer — confirm with PM
WhatsApp Business API feasibility for arbitrary custom messages — confirm with Eng before designing Step 3
Template library model: shared with simulations or red-team-specific? Confirm before designing Screen 0 and Step 3
Individual targeting max user count — confirm with Eng before DS component spec
Compliance Pre-flight step pattern requires DS review and component definition — do not begin Figma on Step 4 until approved
Per-channel coverage indicator (multi-row breakdown) requires DS review
Individual targeting people-search component requires DS review — net-new component
Compliance acknowledgment copy in Step 8 requires legal review before finalization
Sub-role RBAC visibility into red team results — confirm who can see results beyond the launching admin
IDP SCIM integration required for geo/site filter and geo-breakdown chart — confirm availability and timeline with Eng
Domo API integration for automated reporting (Concentrix request) — confirm v3 reporting suite scope with PM
Historical data load (1 year) for repeat-offender tracking — confirm data retention policy and Eng feasibility
Employee ID source — currently placeholder '000' in PDF reports until Workday integration complete; confirm ETA
Daily email alert system architecture — N8N automations built but AWS API connection pending (Concentrix #25, TaskUs #9)
risks: WhatsApp API constraints may force template-only sends — reduces adversarial realism; must be communicated clearly in channel selection and template steps
Compliance Pre-flight as a new DS pattern creates a design dependency — cannot Figma Step 4 until DS approves the pattern
Individual targeting people-search component is net-new — scope and complexity unknown until DS component definition is complete
Suppression default for remediation may conflict with customers who want integrated risk scoring — must be configurable, not hardcoded
8-step wizard is longer than SMS phishing 7-step wizard — test admin completion time; consider if Steps 4 and 5 can be collapsed if compliance pre-flight has no pending items
new_patterns_required: Compliance Pre-flight wizard step — readiness checklist with third-party status cards (carrier, API, legal docs); requires DS review
Per-channel coverage indicator — multi-row breakdown (SMS / WhatsApp / neither) instead of single percentage; requires DS review as new variant
Individual targeting people-search component — search input + results table with per-user channel coverage chips + selected-list panel + overlap detection; requires DS review as net-new component

states.label: Compromised
states.description: Target clicked link, submitted credentials, or took target action

states.label: Declined
states.description: Target refused or reported the message

conversation_states.retag_rule: Red Team admin can move Non-Complicit → Complicit only. Cannot reverse post-launch.

reporting_dashboard.trigger: 33 feedback items from TaskUs and Concentrix (Feb–Apr 2026) confirmed that customers are building reporting dashboards manually from raw data exports. The campaign detail view must replace these external workarounds natively.
overview_tab_components: Stats row: Total targets | SMS delivered | WhatsApp delivered | Total clicked (click rate %) | Not reached
Filter bar: calendar date range picker (not presets) + geo/site filter + campaign AEP filter + Export CSV
Daily activity stacked bar chart: Messages Sent / Ignored / Complicit by day of week
Complicit by campaign horizontal bar chart (human-readable campaign names)
Complicit by site/geo horizontal bar chart (blocked on IDP SCIM — show unavailable state if not integrated)
Channel breakdown: % by SMS / WhatsApp / Viber per geography
rbac_gates: Re-tag Non-Complicit → Complicit: Red Team admin only; standard admin sees disabled control + tooltip
Export CSV: admin roles only; read-only viewer cannot export
Cancel campaign: Red Team admin only
Email alert configuration: Red Team admin only
reporting_dashboard.email_alert_flow: Set up alerts drawer: recipients (multi-email), frequency (daily / per-event), format (summary count / full list). Daily digest sent at configured window. Alert email links back to Conversations tab.

#6 Employee ID + Email + Start Date in table and export
#8 RBAC gate on Non-Complicit → Complicit re-tagging
#9 Email alert button and configuration flow
#13 Transcript link per row in table and export
#14/#30 Red Team as dedicated section/tab (not embedded in Org Insights)
#20 Employee ID placeholder state while Workday integration pending
#23 Campaign status clarity — 'Completed with no data' explained inline
#24 Historical data view — 1-year range in date picker
#25 Automated daily email alerts for complicit users

#16 QA process — Internal process, not product design
#22 Domo API integration — v3 scope, not current design
#32 Self-service AEP creation — separate feature scope

Design Screen 1 (Channel Selection) first — simplest new screen, highest downstream impact
Design Screen 2 (Audience) in single-channel + group-targeting variant first (closest to existing SMS phishing patterns), then layer individual targeting and multi-channel coverage
Design Screen 4 (Compliance Pre-flight) as a standalone component before embedding in the wizard — validate DS pattern in isolation
Open SMS phishing Figma file as structural reference for Steps 5–8 — adapt, do not recreate
Design campaign detail as two-tab view (Overview + Conversations) per reporting dashboard requirements from customer feedback
Confirm IDP SCIM integration status with Eng before designing geo/site chart — design unavailable state as fallback
Confirm Workday integration ETA with PM — design Employee ID placeholder state while pending
